sections.title: 2023 Amendments
sections.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995) in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA. Amendments were also made in Section 7 Packaging.
sections.retrieval_text:
2023 Amendments
level=1
Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995) in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA. Amendments were also made in Section 7 Packaging.

page_spans.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995) in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA. Amendments were also made in Section 7 Packaging.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.parent_title: SECTION A: STANDARD FOR INFANT FORMULA
path_titles: SECTION A: STANDARD FOR INFANT FORMULA
sections.text: This section of the standard applies to infant formula in liquid or powdered form intended for use, where necessary, as a substitute for human milk in meeting the normal nutritional requirements of infants. This section of the standard contains compositional, quality and safety requirements for infant formula. Only products that comply with the criteria laid down in the provisions of this section of this standard would be accepted for marketing as infant formula. No product other than infant formula may be marketed or otherwise represented as suitable for satisfying, by itself, the nutritional requirements of normal healthy infants during the first months of life. The application of this section of the standard should take into account the recommendations made in the International Code of Marketing of Breast -milk Substitutes (WHO, 1981),2 the Global Strategy for Infant and Young Child Feeding and World Health Assembly resolution WHA54.2 (2001).3

sections.title: Product definition
path_titles: SECTION A: STANDARD FOR INFANT FORMULA
sections.text:
Infant formula means a breastmilk substitute specially manufactured to satisfy, by itself, the nutritional requirements of infants during the first months of life up to the introduction of appropriate complementary feeding. The product is so processed by physical means only and so packaged as to prevent spoilage and contamination under all normal conditions of handling, storage and distribution in the country where the product is sold.

2.1.1 Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants means a substitute for human milk or infant formula that complies with Section 2, Description, of the Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991)14 and is specially manufactured to satisfy, by itself, the special nutritional requirements of infants with specific disorders, diseases or medical conditions during the first months of life up to the introduction of appropriate complementary feeding.
2.1.2 See Section A 2.1.2

sections.title: Essential composition
path_titles: SECTION A: STANDARD FOR INFANT FORMULA
sections.text:
Infant formula is a product based on milk of cows or other animals or a mixture thereof and/or other ingredients which have been proven to be suitable for infant feeding. The nutritional safety and adequacy of infant formula shall be scientifically demonstrated to support growth and development of infants. A ll ingredients and food additives shall be gluten free. Infant formula prepared ready for consumption in accordance with instructions of the manufacturer shall contain per 100 ml not less than 60 kcal (250 kJ) and not more than 70 kcal (295 kJ) of energy. Infant formula prepared ready for consumption shall contain per 100 kcal (100 kJ) the following nutrients with the following minimum and maximum or guidance upper levels (GULs),ii as appropriate. The general principles for establishing these levels are identified in Annex II of this standard.

3.1.1 Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants is a product based on ingredients of animal, plant and/or synthetic origin suitable for human consumption. All ingredients and food additives shall be gluten free.
3.1.2 The composition of formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be based on sound medical and nutritional principles. The nutritional safety and adequacy of the formula shall be scientifically demonstrated to support growth and development in the infants for whom it is intended, as appropriate for the specific products and indications. Their use shall be demonstrated by scientific evidence to be beneficial in the dietary management of the infants for whom it is intended.
3.1.3 The energy content and nutrient composition of formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be based on the requirements for infant formula as given in Sections A 3.1.2 and 3.1.3, except for the compositional provisions which must be modified to meet the special nutritional requirements arising from the disease(s), disorder(s) or medical condition(s) for whose dietary management the product is specifically formulated, labelled and presented.
3.1.4 In addition to the requirements in Section 3.1.3 the following requirements shall also be taken into account, where appropriate:

Infant formula is a product based on milk of cows or other animals or a mixture thereof and/or other ingredients which have been proven to be suitable for infant feeding. The nutritional safety and adequacy of infant formula shall be scientifically demonstrated to support growth and development of infants. A ll ingredients and food additives shall be gluten free. Infant formula prepared ready for consumption in accordance with instructions of the manufacturer shall contain per 100 ml not less than 60 kcal (250 kJ) and not more than 70 kcal (295 kJ) of energy. Infant formula prepared ready for consumption shall contain per 100 kcal (100 kJ) the following nutrients with the following minimum and maximum or guidance upper levels (GULs),ii as appropriate. The general principles for establishing these levels are identified in Annex II of this standard.

3.1.1 Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants is a product based on ingredients of animal, plant and/or synthetic origin suitable for human consumption. All ingredients and food additives shall be gluten free.
3.1.2 The composition of formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be based on sound medical and nutritional principles. The nutritional safety and adequacy of the formula shall be scientifically demonstrated to support growth and development in the infants for whom it is intended, as appropriate for the specific products and indications. sections.title: Protein
sections.text: Unit Minimum Maximum GUL g/100 kcal 1.84), 5) 3.0 - g/100 kJ 0.454), 5) 0.7 -  For the purpose of this standard, the calculation of the protein content of the final product prepared ready for consumption should be based on N x 6.25, unless a scientific justification is provided for the use of a different conversion factor for a particular product. The protein levels set in this standard are based on a nitrogen conversion factor of 6.25. The value of 6.38 is generally established as a specific factor appropriate for conversion of nitrogen to protein in other milk products, and the value of 5.71 as a specific factor for conversion of nitrogen to protein in other soy products.  For an equal energy value the formula must contain an available quantity of each essential and semi-essential amino acid at least equal to that contained in the reference protein (breastmilk as defined in Annex I); nevertheless for calculation purposes, the concentrations of tyrosine and phenylalanine may be added together. The concentrations of methionine and cysteine may be added together if the ratio is less than 2:1; in the case that the ratio is between 2:1 and 3:1 the suitability of the formula has to be demonstrated by clinical testing.  Isolated amino acids may be added to infant formula only to improve its nutritional value for infants. Essential and semi-essential amino acids may be added to improve protein quality, only in amounts necessary for that purpose. Only L-forms of amino acids shall be used.  The minimum value applies to cows’ milk protein. For infant formula based on non -cows’ milk protein other minimum values may need to be applied. For infant formula based on soy protein isolate, a minimum value of 2.25  g/100 kcal (0.5 g/100 kJ) applies.  Infant formula based on non-hydrolysed milk protein containing less than 2 g protein/100 kcal and infant formula based on hydrolysed protein containing less than 2.25 g protein/100 kcal should be clinically evaluated.

sections.title: Lipids
sections.text: Total fat6), 7)  Unit Minimum Maximum GUL g/100 kcal 4.4 6.0 -  g/100 kJ 1.05 1.4 -  Commercially hydrogenated oils and fats shall not be used in infant formula.  Lauric and myristic acids are constituents of fats, but combined shall not exceed 20  percent of total fatty acids. The content of trans fatty acids shall not exceed 3 percent of total fatty acids. Trans fatty acids are endogenous components of milkfat. The acceptance of up to 3  percent of trans fatty acids is intended to allow for the use of milkfat in infant formulae. The erucic acid content shall not exceed 1  percent of total fatty acids. The total content of phospholipids should not exceed 300 mg/100 kcal (72 mg/100 kJ). sections.title: Carbohydrates
sections.text:
Total carbohydrates8) 
Unit Minimum Maximum GUL 
g/100 kcal 9.0 14.0 - 
g/100 kJ 2.2 3.3 - 
8) Lactose and glucose polymers should be the preferred carbohydrates in formula based on cows’ milk protein and hydrolysed protein. Only precooked and/or gelatinized starches gluten free by nature may be added to infant formula up to 30 percent of total carbohydrates and up to 2 g/100 ml. 
Sucrose, unless needed, and the addition of fructose as an ingredient, should be avoided in infant formula because of potential life-threatening symptoms in young infants with unrecognized hereditary fructose intolerance.

sections.title: Optional ingredients
sections.text:
Optional ingredients subtitle introduces additional ingredients beyond Section A 3.1.3 with general statements about their use, suitability, and safety.

In addition to the compositional requirements listed under Section 3.1.3, other ingredients may be added in order to provide substances ordinarily found in human milk or required to ensure that the formulation is suitable as the sole source of nutrition for the infant and for the dietary management of his/her disease, disorder or medical condition.

sections.text: The suitability for the intended special medical purpose, the suitability for the particular nutritional use of infants and the safety of these substances shall be scientifically demonstrated. The formula shall contain sufficient amounts of these substances to achieve the intended effect.

sections.title: Fluoride
sections.text:
Fluoride should not be added to infant formula. In any case its level should not exceed 100  µg/100 kcal (24 µg/100 kJ) in infant formula prepared ready for consumption as recommended by the manufacturer.

See Section A 3.4

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Acidity regulators, antioxidants, carriers, emulsifiers, packaging gases and thickeners used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 13.1.3 (Formulae for special medical purposes intended for infants) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. Only the food additives listed in food category 13.1.3 (Formula e for special medical purposes intended for infants) of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 may be present in the foods conforming to this standard, as a result of carry-over from a raw material or other ingredient (including food additive) used to produce the food, subject to the following conditions: a) the amount of the food additive in the raw materials or other ingredients (including food additives) does not exceed the maximum level specified; and b) the food into which the food additive is carried over does not contain the food additive in greater quantity than would be introduced by the use of the raw materials or ingredients under good manufacturing practice, consistent with the provisions on carry -over in the preamble of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995).

Only the food additives listed in food category 13.1.3 (Formula e for special medical purposes intended for infants) of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 may be present in the foods conforming to this standard, as a result of carry-over from a raw material or other ingredient (including food additive) used to produce the food, subject to the following conditions: a) the amount of the food additive in the raw materials or other ingredients (including food additives) does not exceed the maximum level specified; and b) the food into which the food additive is carried over does not contain the food additive in greater quantity than would be introduced by the use of the raw materials or ingredients under good manufacturing practice, consistent with the provisions on carry -over in the preamble of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995).

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).5  
The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residues limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

See Section A 5.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
6.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),6 and other relevant Codex texts such as the Code of Hy gienic Practice for Powdered Formula e for Infants and Young Children (CXC 66-2008).7 
6.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Food (CXG 21-1997).8

See Section A 6.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text:
7.1 The product shall be packed in containers which will safeguard the hygienic and other qualities of the food. When in liquid form, the product shall be packed in hermetically sealed containers. 
7.2 The containers, including packaging materials, shall be made only of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended uses. Where the Codex Alimentarius Commission has established a standard for any such substance used as packaging materials, that standard shall apply.

See Section A 7.

sections.title: FILL OF CONTAINER
sections.text:
In the case of products in ready-to-eat form, the fill of container shall be: (i) not less than 80 percent v/v for products weighing less than 150 g (5 oz); (ii) not less than 85 percent v/v for products in the weight range 150–250 g (5-8 oz); and (iii) not less than 90 percent v/v for products weighing more than 250 g (8 oz) of the water capacity of the container. The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20 °C which the sealed container will hold completely filled.

See Section A 8.

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text:
The requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985),9 the Guidelines on Nutrition Labelling (CXG 2-1985)10 and the Guidelines for Use of Nutrition and Health Claims  (CXG 23-1997)11 apply to infant formula and formula for special medical purposes  for infants. These requirements include a prohibition on the use of nutrition and health claims for foods for infants and young children except where specifically provided for in relevant Codex standards or national legislation. In addition to these requirements the following specific provisions apply:

See introductory paragraph of Section A 9.

sections.title: The name of the food
sections.text:
9.1.1 The text of the label and all other information accompanying the product shall be written in the appropriate language(s). 
9.1.2 The name of the product shall be either "Infant formula" or any appropriate designation indicating the true nature of the product, in accordance with national usage. 
9.1.3 The sources of protein in the product shall be clearly shown on the label.  
9.1.4 If cows’ milk is the only source of protein, the product may be labelled "Infant formula based on cows’ milk". 
9.1.5 A product which contains neither milk nor  any milk derivative shall be labelled "contains no milk or milk products" or an equivalent phrase.

sections.title: List of ingredients
sections.text:
9.2.1 A complete list of ingredients shall be declared on the label in descending order of proportion except that in the case of added vitamins and minerals, these ingredients may be arranged as separate groups for vitamins and mi nerals. Within these groups the vitamins and minerals need not be listed in descending order of proportion. 
9.2.2 The specific name shall be declared for ingredients of animal or plant origin and for food additives. In addition, appropriate class names for these ingredients and additives may be included on the label.

See Section A 9.2

sections.title: Declaration of nutritive value
sections.text:
The declaration of nutrition information shall contain the following information which should be in the following order: 
a) the amount of energy, expressed in kilocalories (kcal) and/or kilojoules (kJ), and the number of grams of protein, carbohydrate and fat per 100 grams or per 100 millilitres of the food as sold as well as per 100 millilitres of the food ready for use, when prepared according to the instructions on the label.

Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be labelled with complete nutrition labelling according to Section 4.2 of Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991).

sections.title: DATE MARKING AND STORAGE INSTRUCTIONS
sections.text:
9.4.1 The date of minimum durability (preceded by the words "best before") shall be declared by the day, month and year in uncoded numerical sequence except that for products with a shelf life of more than three months, the month and year will suffice. The month may be indicated by letters in those countries where such use will not confuse the consumer. In the case of products requiring a declaration of month and year only, and the shelf life of the product is valid to the end of a given year, the expression "end (stated year)" may be used as an alternative.
9.4.2 In addition to the date, any special conditions for the storage of the food shall be indicated if the validity of the date depends thereon. Where practicable, storage instructions shall be in close proximity to the date marking.

See Section A 9.4

sections.title: INFORMATION FOR USE
sections.text:
9.5.1 Products in liquid form may be used either directly or in the case of concentrated liquid products, must be prepared with water that is safe or has been rendered safe by previous boiling before feeding, according to directions for use. Products in powder form should be reconstituted with water that is safe or has been rendered safe by previous boiling for preparation. Adequate directions for the appropriate preparation and handling should be in accordance with good hygienic practice.
9.5.2 Adequate directions for the appropriate preparations and use of the product, including its storage and disposal after preparation, i.e. that formula remaining after feeding should be discarded, shall appear on the label and in any accompanying leaflet.
9.5.3 The label shall carry clear graphic instructions illustrating the method of preparation of the product.
9.5.4 The directions should be accompanied by a warning about the health hazards of inappropriate preparation, storage and use.
9.5.5 Adequate directions regarding the storage of the product after the container has been opened, shall appear on the label and in any accompanying leaflet.

See Section A 9.5

sections.title: ADDITIONAL LABELLING REQUIREMENTS
sections.text:
9.6.1 Labels should not discourage breastfeeding. Each container label shall have a clear, conspicuous and easily readable message which includes the following points:
a) the words "important notice" or their equivalent;
b) the statement "Breastmilk is the best food for your baby" or a similar statement as to the superiority of breastfeeding or breastmilk; and
c) a statement that the product should only be used on advice of an independent health worker as to the need for its use and the proper method of use.
9.6.2 The label shall have no pictures of infants and women nor any other picture or text which idealizes the use of infant formula.
9.6.3 The terms "humanized", "maternalized" or other similar terms shall not be used.
9.6.4 Information shall appear on the label to the effect that infants should receive complementary foods in addition to the formula, from an age that is appropriate for their specific growth and development needs, as advised by an independent health worker, and in any case from the age over 6 months.
9.6.5 The products shall be labelled in such a way as to avoid any risk of confusion between infant formula, follow-up formula, and formula for special medical purposes.

sections.title: Messages on labels
sections.text:
Labels should not discourage breastfeeding. Each container label shall have a clear, conspicuous and easily readable message which includes the following points:
a) the words "important notice" or their equivalent;
b) the statement "Breastmilk is the best food for your baby" or a similar statement as to the superiority of breastfeeding or breastmilk; and
c) a statement that the product should only be used on advice of an independent health worker as to the need for its use and the proper method of use.

sections.title: Complementary feeding information
sections.text: Information shall appear on the label to the effect that infants should receive complementary foods in addition to the formula, from an age that is appropriate for their specific growth and development needs, as advised by an independent health worker, and in any case from the age over 6 months.

sections.title: Labelling distinction
sections.text: The products shall be labelled in such a way as to avoid any risk of confusion between infant formula, follow-up formula, and formula for special medical purposes.

sections.title: ANNEX I
sections.text: Essential and semi-essential amino acids in breastmilk* For the purpose of this standard, the essential and semi-essential amino acids in human milk from published studies which report measurements of the total nitrogen content and/or the calculation method of the protein content, expressed as mg per g of nitrogen and as mg per 100 kcal are listed. The average level of an amino acid (mg per g of nitrogen) from each study was used to calculate the corresponding amino acid content per 100 kcal of an infant formula with the minimum protein content of 1.8 g/100 kcal accepted in this standard (mg amino acid/g nitrogen in breastmilk divided by the nitrogen conversion factor of 6.25 and multiplied by 1.8). The mean of the sums of the average amino acid levels from all studies was converted in the same manner to the average amounts of an amino acid per g of protein (total nitrogen x 6.25) and per 100 kcal of energy (columns 19 and 20 of the table). National authorities may use all of the listed values. * Adapted from Koletzko, Baker, Cleghorn, et al. (2005)13 Lönnerdal and Forsum (1985) Darragh and Moughan (1998) Bindels and Harzer (1985) Janas et al. (1987) Villalpando et al. (1998) Räihä et al. (2002) mod Nayman et al. (1979) Yonekubo et al. sections.title: ANNEX II GENERAL PRINCIPLES FOR ESTABLISHING MINIMUM AND MAXIMUM VALUES FOR THE ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION OF INFANT FORMULA
path_titles: ANNEX II GENERAL PRINCIPLES FOR ESTABLISHING MINIMUM AND MAXIMUM VALUES FOR THE ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION OF INFANT FORMULA
sections.citation: 015c7767-be2c-4bdb-a352-3a8b968aae85, ANNEX II GENERAL PRINCIPLES FOR ESTABLISHING MINIMUM AND MAXIMUM VALUES FOR THE ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION OF INFANT FORMULA, p. 14
sections.text: 1. The goal of establishing minimum and maximum values is to provide safe and nutritionally adequate infant formula products that meet the normal nutritional requirements of infants. 2. A nutritionally adequate infant formula will promote growth and development consistent with science-based standards and meet the nutritional requirements of infants when fed as a sole source of nutrition during the first months of life up to the introduction of appropriate complementary feeding. 3. The values to be established are based on an independent evaluation, in particular of the scientific evidence of the amounts needed to meet the nutritional requirements of infants, considering relevant human infant studies and the composition of breastmilk. 4. In addition to the principles set out in No. 3, when setting minimum and maximum values, consideration will also be given to the safety of such values. For nutrients with a documented risk of adverse health effects the upper levels to be taken into account will be determined using a science-based risk assessment approach. Where scientific data are not sufficient for a science-based risk assessment, consideration should be given to an established history of apparently safe use of the nutrient in infants, as appropriate. Values derived on the basis of meeting the nutritional requirements of infants and an established history of apparently safe use should be considered as interim GULs. The approach to setting maximum and upper guidance values shall be made transparent and comprehensible. 5. When establishing minimum and maximum amounts, the following should also be taken into account: a) bioavailability, processing losses and shelf-life stability from the ingredients and formula matrix; b) total levels of a nutrient in infant formula, taking into account both naturally occurring nutrients in the ingredients and added nutrients; and c) the inherent variability of nutrients in ingredients and in water that may be added to the infant formula during manufacture. 6. Averages for individual nutrients, as appropriate, to ensure that the required minimum levels are met throughout the shelf-life of the formula, will be included in the maximum value. 7. In establishing minimum or maximum amounts of nutrients per 100 kcal (or per 100 kJ) of infant formula based on consideration of reference values for the nutrients expressed as units per daily intake or per kilogram of body weight, the following assumptions will be considered: a) the mean intake of prepared formula for infants from birth to six months of age is 750 ml per day; b) a representative body weight for an infant over this period is 5 kg; and c) a representative caloric intake of an infant over this period is 500 kcal per day (or 100 kcal/kg/day). Modifications of the approach may be needed when there is justification for deviating from one or more of these assumptions with regard to the specific formula product or specific infant population group.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.parent_title: SECTION B: FORMULA FOR SPECIAL MEDICAL PURPOSES INTENDED FOR INFANTS
sections.text:
1.1 This section of the standard applies to formula for special medical purposes intended for infants in liquid or powdered form intended for use, where necessary, as a substitute for human milk or infant formula in meeting the special nutritional requirements arising from the disorder, disease or medical condition for whose dietary management the product has been formulated.
1.2 This section of the standard contains compositional, quality, labelling and safety requirements for formula for special medical purposes intended for infants.
1.3 Only products that comply with the criteria laid down in the provisions of this section of this standard would be accepted for marketing as formula for special medical purposes intended for infants.
1.4 The application of this section of the standard should take into account, as appropriate for the products to which the section applies and the special needs of the infants for whom they are intended, the recommendations made in the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes (WHO, 1981),2 the Global Strategy for Infant and Young Child Feeding and World Health Assembly resolution WHA54.2 (2001).3

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.parent_title: SECTION B: FORMULA FOR SPECIAL MEDICAL PURPOSES INTENDED FOR INFANTS
sections.text:
2.1 Product definition
2.1.1 Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants means a substitute for human milk or infant formula that complies with Section 2, Description, of the Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991)14 and is specially manufactured to satisfy, by itself, the special nutritional requirements of infants with specific disorders, diseases or medical conditions during the first months of life up to the introduction of appropriate complementary feeding.
2.1.2 See Section A 2.1.2
2.2 Other definitions
See Section A 2.2

sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.parent_title: SECTION B: FORMULA FOR SPECIAL MEDICAL PURPOSES INTENDED FOR INFANTS
sections.text:
3.1 Essential composition
3.1.1 Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants is a product based on ingredients of animal, plant and/or synthetic origin suitable for human consumption. All ingredients and food additives shall be gluten free.
3.1.2 The composition of formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be based on sound medical and nutritional principles. The nutritional safety and adequacy of the formula shall be scientifically demonstrated to support growth and development in the infants for whom it is intended, as appropriate for the specific products and indications. Their use shall be demonstrated by scientific evidence to be beneficial in the dietary management of the infants for whom it is intended.
3.1.3 The energy content and nutrient composition of formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be based on the requirements for infant formula as given in Sections A 3.1.2 and 3.1.3, except for the compositional provisions which must be modified to meet the special nutritional requirements arising from the disease(s), disorder(s) or medical condition(s) for whose dietary management the product is specifically formulated, labelled and presented.
3.1.4 In addition to the requirements in Section 3.1.3 the following requirements shall also be taken into account, where appropriate:
Chromium
Unit Minimum Maximum GUL
bcg/100 kcal 1.5 - 10
bcg/100 kJ 0.4 - 2.4

sections.title: NOTES
sections.text:
1 FAO and WHO. 1995. General Standard for Food Additives. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No.  CXS 192-1995. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
2 World Health Organization (WHO). 1981. International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes. Geneva. 
9241541601.pdf (who.int) 
3 WHO and UNICEF. 2003. The Global Strategy for Infant and Young Child Feeding and World Health Assembly resolution WHA54. Geneva. 9241562218.pdf (who.int) 
4 FAO and WHO. 1979. Advisory Lists of Nutrient Compounds for Use in Foods for Special Dietary Uses Intended for Infants and Young Children. Codex Alimentarius Guideline, No. CXG 10-1979. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome.  
5 FAO and WHO. 1995. General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 193-1995 Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
6 FAO and WHO. 1969. General Principles of Food Hygiene. Codex Alimentarius Code of Practice, No. CXC 1-1969. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
7 FAO and WHO. 2008. Code of Hygienic Practice for Powdered Formulae for Infants and Young Children . Codex Alimentarius Code of Practice, No. CXC 66-2008. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
8 FAO and WHO. 1997. Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods. Codex Alimentarius Guideline, No. CXG 21-1997. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
9 FAO and WHO. 1985. General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods . Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 1-1985. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
10 FAO and WHO. 1985. Guidelines on Nutrition Labelling. Codex Alimentarius Guideline, No. CXG 2-1985. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
11 FAO and WHO. 1997. Guidelines for Use of Nutrition and Health Claims . Codex Alimentarius Guideline, No. CXG 23-1997. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
12 FAO and WHO. 1999. Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling . Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 234-1999. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
13 Koletzko B, Baker S, Cleghorn G et al.  2005. Global standard for the composition of infant formula: Recommendations of ESPGHAN coordinated international expert group. Journal of Pediatric Gastroenterology and Nutrition. 2005;41:584–599. 
14 FAO and WHO. 1991. Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes . Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 180-1991. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome.

sentences.original: This section of the standard applies to infant formula in liquid or powdered form intended for use, where necessary, as a substitute for human milk in meeting the normal nutritional requirements of infants.

classification.sentence: The application of this section of the standard should take into account the recommendations made in the International Code of Marketing of Breast -milk Substitutes (WHO, 1981),2 the Global Strategy for Infant and Young Child Feeding and World Health Assembly resolution WHA54.2 (2001).3
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb ('should') + verb phrase + prepositional phrase listing relevant documents

components.Subject: The application of this section of the standard
components.Objects: the recommendations made in the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes (WHO, 1981), the Global Strategy for Infant and Young Child Feeding and World Health Assembly resolution WHA54.2 (2001)

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation via 'should' directed at application practice and specifies consideration of multiple documents, but does not conform to any known template; its advisory nature defines the communicative function and modality clearly, requiring a new structural pattern.
notes: Sentence combines a normative obligation with multiple referenced documents, which is not present in known templates.
The use of 'should' here indicates a recommendation-level obligation rather than a strict mandate, justifying classification under obligation with advisory function.

sentences.original: This section of the standard applies to formula for special medical purposes intended for infants in liquid or powdered form intended for use, where necessary, as a substitute for human milk or infant formula in meeting the special nutritional requirements arising from the disorder, disease or medical condition for whose dietary management the product has been formulated.

classification.sentence: This section of the standard applies to formula for special medical purposes intended for infants in liquid or powdered form intended for use, where necessary, as a substitute for human milk or infant formula in meeting the special nutritional requirements arising from the disorder, disease or medical condition for whose dietary management the product has been formulated.
classification.function: Defining scope with conditional criteria and intended use
classification.pattern: Subject + applies to + Object with extensive postmodification specifying intended users, form, and use conditions

components.Subject: This section of the standard
components.Object: formula for special medical purposes intended for infants in liquid or powdered form intended for use, where necessary, as a substitute for human milk or infant formula in meeting the special nutritional requirements arising from the disorder, disease or medical condition for whose dietary management the product has been formulated

classification.justification: The sentence specifies the applicability scope containing multiple embedded conditions defining product type, intended users, form, and medical context. No existing templates match this complex conditional scope definition, thus a new pattern reflecting a detailed scope/condition sentence was created.
notes: Long and complex conditional scope statement; no close template matches available
Focus on defining applicability with embedded use conditions

sentences.original: No product other than infant formula may be marketed or otherwise represented as suitable for satisfying, by itself, the nutritional requirements of normal healthy infants during the first months of life.

classification.sentence: No product other than infant formula may be marketed or otherwise represented as suitable for satisfying, by itself, the nutritional requirements of normal healthy infants during the first months of life.
classification.pattern: Negation + Exception + Permission + Action + Purpose + Target Population + Time Frame

components.negation: No product other than infant formula
components.permission_action: may be marketed or otherwise represented
components.restriction: as suitable for satisfying, by itself, the nutritional requirements
components.time_frame: during the first months of life

classification.justification: Sentence grants permission only to infant formula to be marketed as suitable for infant nutrition, expressed via negation and exception, indicating a permission with restriction structure. The complex negation combined with permission is not covered by known templates, leading to a new pattern classification.
notes: Sentence combines negation with permission modality, which is less common and complicates template matching.
The phrase 'may be marketed or otherwise represented' bundles permission over multiple related actions, increasing structural complexity.

sentences.original: 2.1.1 Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants means a substitute for human milk or infant formula that complies with Section 2, Description, of the Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991)14 and is specially manufactured to satisfy, by itself, the special nutritional requirements of infants with specific disorders, diseases or medical conditions during the first months of life up to the introduction of appropriate complementary feeding.

sentences.original: Infant formula is a product based on milk of cows or other animals or a mixture thereof and/or other ingredients which have been proven to be suitable for infant feeding.

classification.sentence:
3.1.3 The energy content and nutrient composition of formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be based on the requirements for infant formula as given in Sections A 3.1.2 and 3.1.3, except for the compositional provisions which must be modified to meet the special nutritional requirements arising from the disease(s), disorder(s) or medical condition(s) for whose dietary management the product is specifically formulated, labelled and presented.
3.1.4 In addition to the requirements in Section 3.1.3 the following requirements shall also be taken into account, where appropriate:
Chromium
Unit Minimum Maximum GUL
bcg/100 kcal 1.5 - 10
bcg/100 kJ 0.4 - 2.4

3.1.3 The energy content and nutrient composition of formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be based on the requirements for infant formula as given in Sections A 3.1.2 and 3.1.3, except for the compositional provisions which must be modified to meet the special nutritional requirements arising from the disease(s), disorder(s) or medical condition(s) for whose dietary management the product is specifically formulated, labelled and presented.
3.1.4 In addition to the requirements in Section 3.1.3 the following requirements shall also be taken into account, where appropriate:
Chromium
Unit Minimum Maximum GUL
bcg/100 kcal 1.5 - 10
bcg/100 kJ 0.4 - 2.4

sentences.original: Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants means a substitute for human milk or infant formula that complies with Section 2, Description, of the Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991)14 and is specially manufactured to satisfy, by itself, the special nutritional requirements of infants with specific disorders, diseases or medical conditions during the first months of life up to the introduction of appropriate complementary feeding.

classification.sentence: Sucrose, unless needed, and the addition of fructose as an ingredient, should be avoided in infant formula because of potential life-threatening symptoms in young infants with unrecognized hereditary fructose intolerance.
classification.function: Express prohibition with a conditional exception and justification
classification.pattern: [Subject] [Conditional exception], and [additional subject], should be avoided in [context] because of [reason].

components.second_subject: the addition of fructose as an ingredient

classification.justification: The sentence prohibits the use of sucrose and fructose in infant formula but includes a conditional exception ('unless needed') and a causal justification. The complex combined subject and exception make it a new structural pattern not covered by existing templates. The modality is prohibition based on 'should be avoided' and the communicative function is to issue a prohibition qualified by condition and explanation.
notes: The phrase 'unless needed' introduces a conditional exception which adds complexity.
The sentence combines two subjects linked by 'and' with a shared prohibition, a structure uncommon in typical prohibition templates.

sentences.original: Acidity regulators, antioxidants, carriers, emulsifiers, packaging gases and thickeners used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 13.1.3 (Formulae for special medical purposes intended for infants) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.

classification.sentence: Only the food additives listed in food category 13.1.3 (Formula e for special medical purposes intended for infants) of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 may be present in the foods conforming to this standard, as a result of carry-over from a raw material or other ingredient (including food additive) used to produce the food, subject to the following conditions: a) the amount of the food additive in the raw materials or other ingredients (including food additives) does not exceed the maximum level specified; and b) the food into which the food additive is carried over does not contain the food additive in greater quantity than would be introduced by the use of the raw materials or ingredients under good manufacturing practice, consistent with the provisions on carry-over in the preamble of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995).
classification.function: Grant conditional permission to presence of certain food additives under specific conditions
classification.pattern: Statement granting permission to the presence of specified additives as a result of carry-over, qualified by multiple cumulative conditions expressed in a list format

components.permitted_subject: Only the food additives listed in food category 13.1.3 (Formula e for special medical purposes intended for infants) of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)
components.context: in the foods conforming to this standard
components.permission_basis: may be present as a result of carry-over from raw material or other ingredient (including food additive) used to produce the food
conditions: the amount of the food additive in the raw materials or other ingredients (including food additives) does not exceed the maximum level specified

the food into which the food additive is carried over does not contain the food additive in greater quantity than would be introduced by the use of the raw materials or ingredients under good manufacturing practice, consistent with the provisions on carry-over in the preamble of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission for only specified food additives to be present due to carry-over, but strictly conditioned by two detailed clauses. It expresses permission with specific conditions, in a complex conditional format not represented in the known templates, thus creating a new pattern with permission modality and conditional communicative function.
notes: Long sentence with embedded cumulative conditions; complex conditional permission format

sentences.original:
Only the food additives listed in food category 13.1.1 (Infant formula e) of the above-mentioned standard (CXS 192-1995)1 may be present in the foods conforming to this standard, as a result of carry-over from a raw material or other ingredient (including food additive) used to produce the food, subject to the following conditions:

a) the amount of the food additive in the raw materials or other ingredients (including food additives) does not exceed the maximum level specified; and  b) the food into which the food additive is carried over does not contain the food additive in greater quantity than would be introduced by the use of the raw materials or ingredients under good manufacturing practice, consistent with the provisions on carry -over in the preamble of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995).

classification.sentence: Only the food additives listed in food category 13.1.1 (Infant formula e) of the above-mentioned standard (CXS 192-1995)1 may be present in the foods conforming to this standard, as a result of carry-over from a raw material or other ingredient (including food additive) used to produce the food, subject to the following conditions: a) the amount of the food additive in the raw materials or other ingredients (including food additives) does not exceed the maximum level specified; and  b) the food into which the food additive is carried over does not contain the food additive in greater quantity than would be introduced by the use of the raw materials or ingredients under good manufacturing practice, consistent with the provisions on carry -over in the preamble of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995).
classification.pattern: Statement of permitted element preceded by limitation 'Only', followed by a conditional clause specifying detailed quantitative and procedural conditions in enumerated subpoints

components.permitted_items: food additives listed in food category 13.1.1 (Infant formula e) of standard CXS 192-1995
components.permitted_cause: carry-over from a raw material or other ingredient (including food additive) used to produce the food

conditions.a: the amount of the food additive in the raw materials or other ingredients (including food additives) does not exceed the maximum level specified
conditions.b: the food into which the food additive is carried over does not contain food additive in greater quantity than introduced by raw materials or ingredients under good manufacturing practice consistent with CXS 192-1995 preamble provisions

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission for presence of specific food additives exclusively, limited by detailed conditions expressed in enumerated clauses. Its structure includes a restrictive marker 'Only', a permission modality 'may be present', and multiple embedded conditions. No exact or partial match found in known templates, so a new pattern is made reflecting the complex permission conditioned by quantitative and procedural criteria.
notes: Complex regulatory statement combining permission with detailed conditions expressed as enumerated sub-clauses.
The 'Only' restricts permission to a specific list, while conditions set quantitative and good manufacturing practice limits.
Could be interpreted partially as a Permission with Limits, but structural complexity and enumeration justify new pattern.

classification.justification: The sentence establishes a quantitative limit on the carry-over amount of a substance (food additive) into food, framed as a limit on quantity not exceeding a certain reference under good manufacturing practice and referencing a standard. This fits the Limits and Targets category and modality, with a communicative function of setting quantitative limits. However, the complex structure referencing carry-over and GMP conditions does not match any known templates strongly, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence combines a quantitative limit with a contextual condition and a reference to a standard, forming a hybrid pattern not covered by known templates.

sentences.original:
6.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),6 and other relevant Codex texts such as the Code of Hy gienic Practice for Powdered Formula e for Infants and Young Children (CXC 66-2008).7 
6.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Food (CXG 21-1997).8

See Section A 6.

classification.sentence:
6.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),6 and other relevant Codex texts such as the Code of Hy gienic Practice for Powdered Formula e for Infants and Young Children (CXC 66-2008).7 
6.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Food (CXG 21-1997).8

See Section A 6.
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (recommended/should) + that-clause specifying preparative and compliance actions + references to authoritative standards

components.sentence_6.1_recommendation: recommended that the product be prepared and handled in accordance with appropriate sections of specified standards

components.sentence_6.2_reference: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Food (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentences express normative recommendation and directive for compliance using 'recommended' and 'should', indicating obligation modality; they specify actions and compliance references but do not fit established templates since they combine recommendation and compliance obligations referencing multiple standards, thus scored as a new pattern.
notes: The sentences mix recommendation ('It is recommended') with obligation ('should comply'), which although related to obligations, shows a hybrid structure not covered by common templates.
References to multiple named standards and codes indicate normative compliance but complex structure prevents template matching.

sentences.original:

9.1.3

classification.sentence: A product which contains neither milk nor  any milk derivative shall be labelled "contains no milk or milk products" or an equivalent phrase.
classification.function: Mandate labelling requirement for products without milk or milk derivatives
classification.pattern: A product which contains neither [Substances] shall be labelled [LabelText] or an equivalent phrase

components.subject: A product which contains neither milk nor any milk derivative
components.label_text: "contains no milk or milk products" or an equivalent phrase

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation modality mandating a specific labelling requirement on products lacking certain components; structurally, it combines a subject defined by a negative content condition with a mandated labelling phrase or equivalent, which is not represented in known templates, leading to a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence includes a conditional phrase describing the product content but is primarily a labelling obligation, combining content absence and label text specification, which is a unique structural combination.

sentences.original:
The declaration of nutrition information shall contain the following information which should be in the following order: 
a) the amount of energy, expressed in kilocalories (kcal) and/or kilojoules (kJ), and the number of grams of protein, carbohydrate and fat per 100 grams or per 100 millilitres of the food as sold as well as per 100 millilitres of the food ready for use, when prepared according to the instructions on the label.

Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be labelled with complete nutrition labelling according to Section 4.2 of Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991).

classification.sentence:
The declaration of nutrition information shall contain the following information which should be in the following order: 
a) the amount of energy, expressed in kilocalories (kcal) and/or kilojoules (kJ), and the number of grams of protein, carbohydrate and fat per 100 grams or per 100 millilitres of the food as sold as well as per 100 millilitres of the food ready for use, when prepared according to the instructions on the label.

Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be labelled with complete nutrition labelling according to Section 4.2 of Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991).
classification.function: Mandate detailed labelling content and compliance
classification.pattern: Main clause with 'shall' + obligation details; list of required information items; conditional phrase embedded; separate declarative clause with cross-reference obligation

components.main_obligation: The declaration of nutrition information shall contain the following information
components.information_order: should be in the following order
components.item_a: the amount of energy, expressed in kilocalories (kcal) and/or kilojoules (kJ), and the number of grams of protein, carbohydrate and fat per 100 grams or per 100 millilitres of the food as sold as well as per 100 millilitres of the food ready for use, when prepared according to the instructions on the label
components.additional_obligation: Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants shall be labelled with complete nutrition labelling according to Section 4.2 of Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991)

classification.justification: The sentence contains multiple normative obligations about labelling nutrition information with a detailed list, order requirement, and a cross-reference to another standard. The complex structure with nested requirements and references does not match any known simple template, so a new pattern is defined capturing obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: Multi-part normative statement combining specific numeric info requirements, ordering, and cross-reference obligation.
Contains a conditional phrase describing preparation instructions, but overall obligation modality dominates.
No exact known template match in the empty catalog, hence new pattern.

sentences.original:
Each container label shall have a clear, conspicuous and easily readable message which includes the following points:
a) the words "important notice" or their equivalent;
b) the statement "Breastmilk is the best food for your baby" or a similar statement as to the superiority of breastfeeding or breastmilk; and
c) a statement that the product should only be used on advice of an independent health worker as to the need for its use and the proper method of use.

sentences.original: The date of minimum durability (preceded by the words "best before") shall be declared by the day, month and year in uncoded numerical sequence except that for products with a shelf life of more than three months, the month and year will suffice.

classification.sentence: In the case of products requiring a declaration of month and year only, and the shelf life of the product is valid to the end of a given year, the expression "end (stated year)" may be used as an alternative.
classification.function: Granting permission for alternative expression use under specified conditions
classification.pattern: Conditional clause (condition on product marking and shelf life) + main clause granting permission for specific expression usage

components.condition: products requiring a declaration of month and year only, and the shelf life of the product is valid to the end of a given year
components.permission_granted: the expression "end (stated year)" may be used as an alternative

classification.justification: The sentence combines a conditional clause specifying product labeling and shelf life criteria with a permission modality granting use of a particular expression. It does not strongly match any existing known templates in the catalog, so a new pattern reflecting conditional permission for alternate expression use is created.
notes: Sentence involves both a compound condition and permission, which is a complex pattern not covered by existing templates.

sentences.original: In addition to the date, any special conditions for the storage of the food shall be indicated if the validity of the date depends thereon.

classification.sentence: Where practicable, storage instructions shall be in close proximity to the date marking.
classification.function: Mandate of placement of storage instructions relative to date marking
classification.pattern: [Condition adverbial], [Obligation statement with placement requirement]

components.obligation: storage instructions shall be in close proximity to the date marking

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation regarding the location of storage instructions with a condition modifier 'Where practicable'; no existing template closely fits this specific conditional placement mandate, requiring a new pattern with an obligation modality and a condition adverbial.
notes: The conditional phrase 'Where practicable' functions as an adverbial qualifier, which is less common in strict obligation templates.
The structure combines a conditional modifier with an obligation about spatial arrangement rather than an action or procedural requirement, distinguishing it from typical obligation templates.

sentences.original: Labels should not discourage breastfeeding.

classification.sentence:
Each container label shall have a clear, conspicuous and easily readable message which includes the following points:
a) the words "important notice" or their equivalent;
b) the statement "Breastmilk is the best food for your baby" or a similar statement as to the superiority of breastfeeding or breastmilk; and
c) a statement that the product should only be used on advice of an independent health worker as to the need for its use and the proper method of use.
classification.pattern: Subject + shall have + object + which includes + enumerated list of required elements

components.subject: Each container label
components.required_message_qualities: a clear, conspicuous and easily readable message
included_points: the words "important notice" or their equivalent
the statement "Breastmilk is the best food for your baby" or a similar statement as to the superiority of breastfeeding or breastmilk
a statement that the product should only be used on advice of an independent health worker as to the need for its use and the proper method of use

classification.justification: The sentence imposes a clear obligation on container labels to include a specific message with enumerated required elements, which matches an obligation modality and requirement specification function. However, no existing template in the catalog matches the complex enumerated structure and specific content requirements, thus a new pattern with a structural description reflecting the enumeration is created.
notes: Enumerated list of required message points introduces complexity not covered by simple obligation templates.
Includes detailed content specification and conditional advice embedded in component c).

sentences.original: In establishing minimum or maximum amounts of nutrients per 100 kcal (or per 100 kJ) of infant formula based on consideration of reference values for the nutrients expressed as units per daily intake or per kilogram of body weight, the following assumptions will be considered: a) the mean intake of prepared formula for infants from birth to six months of age is 750 ml per day; b) a representative body weight for an infant over this period is 5 kg; and c) a representative caloric intake of an infant over this period is 500 kcal per day (or 100 kcal/kg/day).

sentences.original: 1.1 This section of the standard applies to formula for special medical purposes intended for infants in liquid or powdered form intended for use, where necessary, as a substitute for human milk or infant formula in meeting the special nutritional requirements arising from the disorder, disease or medical condition for whose dietary management the product has been formulated.

classification.sentence: 1.4 The application of this section of the standard should take into account, as appropriate for the products to which the section applies and the special needs of the infants for whom they are intended, the recommendations made in the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes (WHO, 1981),2 the Global Strategy for Infant and Young Child Feeding and World Health Assembly resolution WHA54.2 (2001).3
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb 'should' + verb phrase + contextual qualifiers + direct object (recommendations)

components.subject: The application of this section of the standard
components.contextual_qualifiers: as appropriate for the products to which the section applies and the special needs of the infants for whom they are intended
components.direct_object: the recommendations made in the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes (WHO, 1981), the Global Strategy for Infant and Young Child Feeding and World Health Assembly resolution WHA54.2 (2001)

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation through 'should' directing a specific action (taking into account recommendations) with detailed contextual qualifiers; it does not strongly match any known templates so a new pattern is created reflecting recommendation incorporation under obligation modality.
notes: The sentence's complex qualifiers and reference to multiple external documents complicate matching to simpler templates.
Use of 'should' in normative context indicates obligation rather than mere permission.

sentences.original:
2.1 Product definition
2.1.1 Formula for special medical purposes intended for infants means a substitute for human milk or infant formula that complies with Section 2, Description, of the Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991)14 and is specially manufactured to satisfy, by itself, the special nutritional requirements of infants with specific disorders, diseases or medical conditions during the first months of life up to the introduction of appropriate complementary feeding.

classification.sentence:
2.1.2 See Section A 2.1.2
2.2 Other definitions
See Section A 2.2

It does not match any known templates. The structure involves a clause number, a directive 'See', and a section reference, forming a new pattern of normative cross-reference instruction.
notes: This sentence mainly consists of citations directing the reader to look at other sections; it lacks obligation, prohibition, or condition typical in regulatory templates.

sentences.original: 3.1 Essential composition

classification.sentence:
In addition to the requirements in Section 3.1.3 the following requirements shall also be taken into account, where appropriate:
Chromium
Unit Minimum Maximum GUL

pages.title: STANDARD FOR INFANT FORMULA AND FORMULAS FOR SPECIAL MEDICAL PURPOSES INTENDED FOR INFANTS
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento e cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto interno relevante para estruturação.
Página contém um título principal de seção '2023 Amendments' como nível 1 e texto explicativo associado. Cabeçalho da página identificado como 'CXS 72-1981 2'. Não há continuidade entre páginas.

Página contém a seção principal 'SECTION A: STANDARD FOR INFANT FORMULA' com várias subseções numeradas conforme padrão. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado como 'CXS 72-1981 3'. A seção 3 'ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS' indica continuar na próxima página.

A página 4 mostra continuação da seção 3, com subseções numeradas 'a) Protein' e 'b) Lipids'. Ambas apresentam tabelas e notas explicativas relacionadas à composição nutricional da fórmula infantil. O cabeçalho da página é 'CXS 72-1981 4'. Não há novos títulos principais ou subseções para níveis inferiores identificados nesta página. Os conteúdos das subseções estão claramente delimitados e não continuam para a próxima página.

Página 5 continua as seções numeradas c) Carbohydrates e d) Vitamins iniciadas na página anterior. Ambas as seções apresentam tabelas e notas explicativas relacionadas à composição nutricional da fórmula infantil. Não há subseções adicionais nesta página. O conteúdo das seções está completo nesta página, sem continuidade para a próxima página.

A página 6 contém a continuidade da seção 'Vitamins' iniciada na página anterior, apresentando várias vitaminas com suas unidades e níveis mínimos, máximos e GULs. A seção 'Minerals and trace elements' inicia nesta página, com o subsequente subtítulo 'Iron'. Não há continuação para a próxima página.

pages.title: CXS 72-1981
pages.summary: Página 7 contém a continuação da seção 'Minerals and trace elements', iniciada na página anterior, apresentando a listagem de minerais com suas unidades e valores mínimos, máximos e GULs. Não há subtítulos estruturais claros nesta página. A seção continua da página anterior e não se estende para a próxima.

Página 8 contém a seção 3.2 'Optional ingredients' iniciando e detalhando seus subitens 3.2.1 a 3.2.3 com descrições e listagens de substâncias. A seção 3.2 continua da página anterior e não se estende para a próxima.

A página 9 continua a seção 3.2 'Optional ingredients' com o item 3.2.4 e apresenta as seções principais 3.3 a 5, incluindo subseções detalhadas nas seções 4.1 e 4.2 com seus subitens a e b. Todos os títulos estão visíveis na página, exceto o início da seção 3.2 que continua da página anterior.

A página 10 contém as seções principais 6 a 9 com seus respectivos textos. A seção 9 LABELLING inclui as subseções 9.1 The name of the food, 9.2 List of ingredients e 9.3 Declaration of nutritive value, esta última continua para a próxima página. Os títulos estão visíveis e bem definidos, sem indícios de continuidade vinda da página anterior.

A página 11 contém as seções 9.4 a 9.6 do documento, com seus respectivos subtítulos numerados e textos explicativos. Não há indicação de continuidade vinda da página anterior para estas seções. A seção 9.6 continua na próxima página, conforme indicado em continues_on_next_page=true, pois o conteúdo 9.6.5 termina próximo ao fim da página, sugerindo continuidade.

A página 12 contém o anexo 'ANNEX I' em nível 1, com um extenso texto explicativo e tabela de dados relacionados aos aminoácidos essenciais e semi-essenciais no leite materno. Não há subdivisões numeradas nesta página e não há continuidade de seção de páginas anteriores nem continuidade para próximas páginas.

A página contém uma única seção 'REFERENCES' em nível 1, que lista as referências bibliográficas. Não há títulos numerados ou subdivisões. O texto é uma lista de citações, sem continuidade para outras páginas.

A página apresenta um único título em nível 1, que é o Anexo II com princípios gerais para o estabelecimento de valores mínimos e máximos para a composição essencial da fórmula infantil. O texto consiste em princípios numerados relacionados à composição. Não há subdivisões claras internas nesta página.

A página apresenta a seção B do documento, contendo os títulos principais 1 (SCOPE), 2 (DESCRIPTION) e 3 (ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS), com suas subseções enumeradas e texto descritivo associado. A seção 1 segue da página anterior (continuidade) e a seção 3 continua na próxima página. A hierarquia está estruturada até o nível 3, com texto interno limpo e sem cabeçalhos repetidos. Mapeamento conservador para uso em RAG.

Página 16 do documento apresenta seções principais numeradas 3.2 a 9, com seus respectivos títulos e textos associados ou referências a outras seções. Há uma hierarquia clara com níveis 1, 2 e 3 para subseções detalhadas. Não há indicação de continuidade da página anterior, e as seções não parecem continuar na próxima página. O texto está limpo, sem cabeçalhos ou rodapés repetitivos, cumprindo requisitos para RAG.

pages.title: CXS 72-1981
pages.summary: Página com seções hierarquicamente organizadas 9 e 10, com subseções e textos associados ou referências. A estrutura segue a numeração indicada com títulos claros e texto relevante para cada nó. Não há continuidade para páginas adjacentes.

A página contém uma seção principal 'NOTES' em nível 1, que lista várias referências numeradas. Não há subseções detectadas. Todo o texto listado pertence a esta seção. Não há continuidade com outras páginas.
